nodes.name: 山地森林
nodes.summary: 以成熟阔叶林和混交林为主的山地森林系统。

nodes.name: Vilcabamba spinetail
nodes.summary: Vilcabamba spinetail（Vilcabamba spinetail，学名 Cranioleuca weskei），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联4个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括山地森林，主要威胁包括森林砍伐。

nodes.name: 森林砍伐
nodes.summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。

nodes.name: Mantaro Valley
nodes.summary: Mantaro Valley是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联2种鸟类，包括White-eared puffbird、Vilcabamba spinetail，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 库斯科大区
nodes.summary: 库斯科大区是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联45种鸟类，包括高山斑䳍、闪羽蜂鸟、秘鲁斑尾蜂鸟等45种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 胡宁大区
nodes.summary: 胡宁大区是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联24种鸟类，包括高山斑䳍、秘魯鸊鷉、Puna plover等24种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 阿亚库乔大区
nodes.summary: 阿亚库乔大区是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联18种鸟类，包括高山斑䳍、须蜂鸟、Lafresnaye's piculet等18种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The Vilcabamba spinetail is found in southeastern Peru, in the Vilcabamba Mountains of western Cuzco Department, in the Mantaro Valley of southeastern Junín Department, and in northern Ayacucho Department

links.evidence: The Vilcabamba spinetail is found in southeastern Peru, in the Vilcabamba Mountains of western Cuzco Department, in the Mantaro Valley of southeastern Junín Department, and in northern Ayacucho Department

links.evidence: The Vilcabamba spinetail is found in southeastern Peru, in the Vilcabamba Mountains of western Cuzco Department, in the Mantaro Valley of southeastern Junín Department, and in northern Ayacucho Department

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The Vilcabamba spinetail is found in southeastern Peru, in the Vilcabamba Mountains of western Cuzco Department, in the Mantaro Valley of southeastern Junín Department, and in northern Ayacucho Department

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: It inhabits the understory of humid evergreen montane forest, where it favors areas with thickets of Chusquea bamboo

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: "The primary threat to this species is deforestation, as it is feared to be susceptible to forest fragmentation and edge effects."
